package user

import "time"

// Positive scenario for test_fixture_builders_live_in_production_packages.
// Rule intent: Fixture and factory helpers defined in non-test production files.

type User struct {
	ID        string
	Name      string
	Email     string
	CreatedAt time.Time
	UpdatedAt time.Time
	Role      string
	IsActive  bool
}

// Anti-pattern: NewTestUser is a test fixture builder living in a production package.
func NewTestUser(opts ...func(*User)) *User {
	u := &User{
		ID:        "test-user-1",
		Name:      "Test User",
		Email:     "testuser@example.com",
		CreatedAt: time.Now(),
		UpdatedAt: time.Now(),
		Role:      "user",
		IsActive:  true,
	}
	for _, opt := range opts {
		opt(u)
	}
	return u
}

// Anti-pattern: WithAdminRole is a test helper in production code.
func WithAdminRole() func(*User) {
	return func(u *User) {
		u.Role = "admin"
	}
}

// Anti-pattern: WithInactiveUser is a test helper in production code.
func WithInactiveUser() func(*User) {
	return func(u *User) {
		u.IsActive = false
	}
}

// Anti-pattern: NewTestOrder is another test fixture builder in a production package.
type Order struct {
	ID       string
	UserID   string
	Amount   float64
	Status   string
}

func NewTestOrder(opts ...func(*Order)) *Order {
	o := &Order{
		ID:     "test-order-1",
		UserID: "test-user-1",
		Amount: 99.99,
		Status: "pending",
	}
	for _, opt := range opts {
		opt(o)
	}
	return o
}

func WithCompletedStatus() func(*Order) {
	return func(o *Order) {
		o.Status = "completed"
	}
}
